message:
🌱 Add lima: Support for FreeBSD guests ? mission (#1155)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,76 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "lima-1508-support-for-freebsd-guests",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "lima: Support for FreeBSD guests ?",
+    "description": "Support for FreeBSD guests ?. Requested by 12+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current lima setup",
+        "description": "Verify your lima version and configuration:\n```bash\nlimactl --version\n```\nThis feature requires a working lima installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review lima configuration",
+        "description": "Review the relevant lima configuration:\n### Description\n\nThere was some question in Slack, about supporting also FreeBSD guests.\n\nI guess it would be \"freema\" or something, and not just \"lima\", but it is doable..."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Support for FreeBSD guests ?",
+        "description": "* https://samuel.karp.dev/blog/2021/05/running-freebsd-jails-with-containerd-1-5/\n\n* https://productionwithscissors.run/2022/09/04/containerd-linux-on-freebsd/\n\nThere is no systemd on BSD, so it needs to use `/var/run`.\n\nGNU bash is in `/usr/local/bin/bash`, default is `/bin/csh`.\n\n~~Eventually it\n```yaml\n# OS: \"Linux\", \"FreeBSD\".\n# 🟢 Builtin default: \"Linux\"\nos: null\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected.\nConfirm the feature described in \"Support for FreeBSD guests ?\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "* https://samuel.karp.dev/blog/2021/05/running-freebsd-jails-with-containerd-1-5/\n\n* https://productionwithscissors.run/2022/09/04/containerd-linux-on-freebsd/\n\nThere is no systemd on BSD, so it needs to use `/var/run`.\n\nGNU bash is in `/usr/local/bin/bash`, default is `/bin/csh`.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "# OS: \"Linux\", \"FreeBSD\".\n# 🟢 Builtin default: \"Linux\"\nos: null",
+        "os: \"FreeBSD\"\n\nimages:\n- location: https://download.freebsd.org/ftp/snapshots/VM-IMAGES/14.0-CURRENT/amd64/Latest/FreeBSD-14.0-CURRENT-amd64.qcow2.xz\n  arch: \"x86_64\"\n- location: https://download.freebsd.org/ftp/snapshots/VM-IMAGES/14.0-CURRENT/aarch64/Latest/FreeBSD-14.0-CURRENT-arm64-aarch64.qcow2.xz\n  arch: \"aarch64\"\n\nvideo:\n  display: \"default\"",
+        "sudo mkdir -p /mnt/lima-cidata\n   sudo mount -t cd9660 /dev/cd0 /mnt/lima-cidata\n   sudo cp /mnt/lima-cidata/meta-data /run/lima-ssh-ready\n   sudo install -m 755 /mnt/lima-cidata/lima-guestagent /usr/local/bin/lima-guestagent\n   sudo -b lima-guestagent daemon\n   sudo cp /mnt/lima-cidata/meta-data /run/lima-boot-done"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "lima",
+      "incubating",
+      "app-definition",
+      "feature"
+    ],

+    ],

+    ],

+    },

+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },

+      "limactl"
+    ],

+  },
